mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the risk-free discount rate, directly increasing the present value of equity cash flows and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate falls below 5.5%, labor market tightness accelerates wage growth and consumer spending, boosting corporate earnings expectations and pushing S&P 500 returns higher over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in more than 60% of instances where UNRATE crosses below 5.5% within a 3-month window.

mechanism_narrative: Strong industrial production growth combined with rising residential housing starts signals synchronized real-sector expansion, which historically precedes earnings upgrades and positive equity returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal of rising INDPRO and HOUST fails to predict positive 60-day forward returns in more than 55% of out-of-sample periods.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of elevated inflation, rising consumer prices erode real purchasing power and trigger anticipated monetary tightening, which compresses equity multiples and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the following week.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL increases do not produce negative 5-day forward returns in more than 60% of high-inflation observations.

mechanism_narrative: A persistent increase in the industrial production index reflects expanding manufacturing capacity and robust aggregate supply, which supports corporate profit margins and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: INDPRO trends fail to produce positive 5-day forward returns in more than 55% of out-of-sample periods.

mechanism_narrative: When real consumer spending, proxied by retail and food services sales, accelerates beyond its 12-month moving average, it signals strong aggregate demand that lifts corporate revenues and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: RSAFS acceleration fails to predict positive 60-day forward returns in more than 60% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: Rapid employment growth coupled with a declining unemployment rate confirms labor market healing, which reduces wage-push inflation risks while boosting consumer demand, jointly driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal of rising PAYEMS and falling UNRATE fails to predict positive 20-day forward returns in more than 55% of out-of-sample periods.

mechanism_narrative: In a supply-constrained macroeconomic environment, sustained declines in industrial production indicate capacity bottlenecks and rising input costs, which compress corporate margins and drive negative S&P 500 forward returns over the following week.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines do not produce negative 5-day forward returns in more than 60% of supply-constrained observations.
